payload.title: Hub should emit rich SSE events for CLI-driven changes (fs-watch)
payload.description:
Today the hub's fs-watch fallback (src/workspaces.js) emits a generic { type: 'external', source: 'fs-watch' } event whenever a sibling process (CLI subprocess) writes to the SQLite DB. The browser has no way to know what actually changed, so the live activity feed shows a useless 'external' toast.

Fix: the fs-watch handler should diff ticket_history since the previous data_version and emit one synthetic { type: 'ticket.updated' | 'ticket.created' | 'comment.added', id, field, old_value, new_value, changed_by, workspace } event per row, matching the shape of in-process emits from repo.js. Then the browser's pushLiveFeed renders proper rich toasts identically for HTTP-driven and CLI-driven activity.

Workaround currently in src/web/app.js: on 'external' event, the client fetches /api/history?limit=10 and replays rows it hasn't seen yet. That's a wasted round-trip per CLI change and misses comment.added (comments aren't in ticket_history). Remove the workaround once this lands.
